document_title: Diploma in Teacher Education - Christian Religious Education (CRE) Curriculum

sub_strands.name: 1.1 Meaning of Christianity
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the meaning of Christianity
Identify key beliefs and practices of Christianity
Appreciate the role of Christianity in personal and societal development
suggested_learning_experiences: Discussion on the definition and origin of Christianity.
Compare core doctrines across major Christian denominations (Catholic, Protestant, Orthodox).
Reflective writing on how Christian values influence moral decision-making.

sub_strands.name: 1.2 The Bible as a Sacred Text
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe the structure and composition of the Bible
Explain the importance of the Bible in Christian life
Demonstrate skills in locating passages and using reference tools
suggested_learning_experiences: Map out the books of the Old and New Testaments.
Practice using concordances, commentaries, and digital Bibles.
Discuss the inspiration, authority, and relevance of Scripture.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 1.3 God the Creator
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the concept of God as Creator according to Genesis 1 and 2
Compare the two creation accounts
Appreciate the environment as God’s creation
suggested_learning_experiences: Read and compare Genesis 1 and 2, identifying similarities and differences.
Debate the relationship between science and biblical creation narratives.
Plan a lesson on stewardship of creation for primary learners.

modules.strand: 2.0 The Life and Ministry of Jesus Christ

sub_strands.name: 2.1 Birth and Early Life of Jesus
specific_learning_outcomes: Narrate the events surrounding the birth of Jesus
Analyse the significance of Jesus’ early life
Demonstrate how to teach the nativity story to children
suggested_learning_experiences: Read Luke 1–2 and Matthew 1–2; identify key figures (Mary, Joseph, angels, shepherds, Magi).
Create dramatizations or storytelling scripts for young learners.
Design Christmas-themed lessons integrating art and music.

sub_strands.name: 2.2 Teachings of Jesus
specific_learning_outcomes: Summarize the main teachings of Jesus (e.g., Sermon on the Mount, parables)
Interpret selected parables and miracles
Apply Jesus' teachings to contemporary moral issues
suggested_learning_experiences: Study parables such as the Good Samaritan, Prodigal Son, and Mustard Seed.
Role-play applications of Jesus’ teachings in modern school and family contexts.
Facilitate group discussions on forgiveness, love of enemies, and humility.

sub_strands.name: 2.3 Miracles of Jesus
specific_learning_outcomes: Narrate selected miracles performed by Jesus
Explain the purpose and significance of the miracles
Develop strategies for teaching miracle stories to learners
suggested_learning_experiences: List and categorize miracles (healings, nature, exorcisms, resurrections).
Analyse the theological meaning behind specific miracles (e.g., calming the storm, feeding the 5000).
Design illustrated story cards or digital presentations for classroom use.

sub_strands.name: 2.4 Suffering, Death, and Resurrection of Jesus
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe the events of Jesus’ passion, death, and resurrection
Explain the meaning of salvation through Christ’s sacrifice
Appreciate the suffering of Jesus and respond to the call of salvation
suggested_learning_experiences: Read the passion narrative from one Gospel account.
Create a Holy Week calendar with daily reflections.
Lead a simulated Lenten devotion or Easter service.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to describe the passion, death and resurrection of Jesus Christ

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Describes in detail the passion, death and resurrection of Jesus Christ
levels.Meets Expectation: Describes the passion, death and resurrection of Jesus Christ
levels.Approaches Expectation: Describes the passion, death and resurrection of Jesus Christ but omits minor details
levels.Below Expectation: Describes the passion, death and resurrection of Jesus Christ but omits major details

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to appreciate the suffering of Jesus on the cross by responding to the call of salvation

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Deeply appreciates the suffering of Jesus on the cross and actively responds to the call of salvation
levels.Meets Expectation: Appreciates the suffering of Jesus on the cross and responds to the call of salvation
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially appreciates the suffering of Jesus on the cross and shows limited response to the call of salvation
levels.Below Expectation: Shows minimal appreciation of the suffering of Jesus on the cross and does not demonstrate a response to the call of salvation

sub_strands.name: 3.1 Coming of the Holy Spirit
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe the coming of the Holy Spirit on the day of Pentecost
Elaborate the events that took place on the day of Pentecost
Analyse the role of the Holy Spirit in the life of a believer
suggested_learning_experiences: Read Acts 2:1–47 and outline the sequence of events.
Discuss the symbolism of wind, fire, and speaking in tongues.
Invite a church leader to speak about the work of the Holy Spirit today.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to describe the coming of the Holy Spirit

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Describes the coming of the Holy Spirit in details
levels.Meets Expectation: Describes the coming of the Holy Spirit
levels.Approaches Expectation: Describes the coming of the Holy Spirit but omits minor details
levels.Below Expectation: Describes the coming of the Holy Spirit but omits major details

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to elaborate the events on the day of Pentecost

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Elaborates the events that took place on the day of Pentecost sequentially
levels.Meets Expectation: Elaborates the events that took place on the day of Pentecost
levels.Approaches Expectation: Elaborates the events that took place on the day of Pentecost with some omissions
levels.Below Expectation: Elaborates the events that took place on the day of Pentecost with major omissions

sub_strands.name: 3.2 Gifts and Fruits of the Holy Spirit
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify the gifts and fruits of the Holy Spirit
Explain the importance of spiritual gifts in the Church
Demonstrate how to nurture the fruits of the Spirit in daily life
suggested_learning_experiences: Study Romans 12, 1 Corinthians 12, and Galatians 5:22–23.
Match spiritual gifts to practical ministry roles.
Create posters or digital infographics on the fruits of the Spirit.

sub_strands.name: 4.1 Christian Values
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify Christian moral values (love, honesty, humility, respect, responsibility)
Explain the importance of living by Christian values
Model Christian values in personal and professional conduct
suggested_learning_experiences: Define each value and give real-life examples.
Analyse case studies involving ethical dilemmas in schools.
Develop a personal code of conduct based on Christian ethics.

sub_strands.name: 4.2 Sin and Repentance
specific_learning_outcomes: Define sin and its consequences
Explain the process of repentance and forgiveness
Encourage a spirit of reconciliation among learners
suggested_learning_experiences: Study biblical examples of sin and restoration (e.g., Adam and Eve, David, Peter).
Write personal reflections on experiences of wrongdoing and forgiveness.
Role-play scenarios where students apologize and make amends.

sub_strands.name: 4.3 Christian Stewardship
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the concept of stewardship (time, talent, treasure)
Promote responsible use of resources in school and community
Organize a project demonstrating Christian stewardship
suggested_learning_experiences: Calculate personal time usage and set improvement goals.
Plan a fundraising or environmental conservation activity.
Discuss tithing and offerings in both church and societal contexts.

sub_strands.name: 5.1 Meaning and Importance of Prayer
specific_learning_outcomes: Define prayer and its purposes
Demonstrate different forms of prayer (adoration, confession, thanksgiving, supplication)
Encourage regular prayer habits among learners
suggested_learning_experiences: Teach the ACTS model of prayer.
Maintain a personal prayer journal for one week.
Lead short class devotions incorporating structured prayer.

sub_strands.name: 5.2 Forms of Worship
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify various forms of Christian worship (liturgical, charismatic, informal)
Compare worship practices across denominations
Plan and lead a simple worship service
suggested_learning_experiences: Attend different church services and report on their styles.
Create orders of service for Sunday school or assembly.
Incorporate music, scripture reading, prayer, and sermon in a mock service.

sub_strands.name: 5.3 Sacraments
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the meaning and significance of baptism and the Lord's Supper
Compare sacramental views across Christian traditions
Prepare a lesson on sacraments for primary learners
suggested_learning_experiences: Study Matthew 28:19 and 1 Corinthians 11:23–26.
Watch videos of baptism and communion services.
Design child-friendly explanations and activities about the sacraments.

sub_strands.name: 6.1 The Nature and Mission of the Church
specific_learning_outcomes: Define the Church as the body of Christ
Explain the mission of the Church (worship, fellowship, discipleship, evangelism, service)
Appreciate the unity of the universal Church
suggested_learning_experiences: Study 1 Corinthians 12 and Ephesians 4 on the body of Christ.
Map local churches and parachurch organizations in the community.
Discuss ecumenism and interdenominational cooperation.

sub_strands.name: 6.2 Leadership in the Church
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify roles and responsibilities of church leaders (pastors, elders, deacons)
Analyse biblical qualifications for leadership
Demonstrate servant leadership in school and community
suggested_learning_experiences: Study pastoral epistles (1 & 2 Timothy, Titus).
Interview a church leader about their calling and duties.
Practice leading small groups with humility and care.

sub_strands.name: 6.3 Christian Family Life
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the Christian understanding of marriage and family
Discuss roles and responsibilities of family members
Model a lesson on how to address challenges facing families today
suggested_learning_experiences: Study Ephesians 5–6 and Colossians 3 on family relationships.
Discuss modern family issues (divorce, single parenting, abuse).
Develop guidance materials for nurturing Christian homes.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to model a lesson on how to address challenges facing families today

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Effectively models a comprehensive lesson addressing multiple challenges facing families today
levels.Meets Expectation: Models a lesson on how to address challenges facing families today
levels.Approaches Expectation: Models a lesson on how to address challenges facing families today with minor gaps
levels.Below Expectation: Models a lesson on how to address challenges facing families today with major gaps

sub_strands.name: 7.1 Lesson Planning for CRE
specific_learning_outcomes: Develop a lesson plan for a CRE topic
Integrate biblical content, learner activities, and assessment
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyse sample CRE lesson plans from approved syllabi.
Draft a lesson plan using the standard format including objectives, resources, introduction, development, conclusion, and evaluation.
Peer-review CRE lesson plans for clarity, engagement, and doctrinal accuracy.

sub_strands.name: 7.2 Storytelling and Drama in CRE
specific_learning_outcomes: Use storytelling techniques to teach Bible stories
Incorporate drama and role play in CRE lessons
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice expressive reading and voice modulation for storytelling.
Adapt Bible stories into short skits for performance.
Deliver a micro-lesson using drama to convey a moral lesson.

sub_strands.name: 7.3 Use of ICT in Teaching CRE
specific_learning_outcomes: Use digital devices and resources to enhance CRE teaching
Apply ethical guidelines when using digital content
suggested_learning_experiences: Create PowerPoint presentations or videos on CRE topics.
Use online Bible tools (e.g., BibleGateway, Blue Letter Bible) for research.
Discuss copyright and plagiarism in digital resource use.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to use digital devices and resources to enhance teaching of CRE

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively uses digital devices and resources to enhance teaching of CRE
levels.Meets Expectation: Uses digital devices and resources to enhance teaching of CRE
levels.Approaches Expectation: Uses digital devices and resources to enhance teaching of CRE with minor errors
levels.Below Expectation: Uses digital devices and resources to enhance teaching of CRE with major errors

sub_strands.name: 7.4 Assessment in CRE
specific_learning_outcomes: Use varied methods to assess learners' understanding of CRE
Provide feedback that encourages spiritual growth
suggested_learning_experiences: Design quizzes, projects, and reflective journals for assessment.
Practice giving constructive verbal and written feedback.
Develop rubrics for evaluating CRE assignments and presentations.

sub_strands.name: 8.1 Interfaith Dialogue
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the importance of interfaith dialogue
Identify commonalities and differences between Christianity and other religions
Promote peaceful coexistence among religious groups
suggested_learning_experiences: Compare core beliefs of Christianity, Islam, Hinduism, Buddhism, and African Traditional Religions.
Organize a panel discussion or debate on religious tolerance.
Visit places of worship of other faiths (mosque, temple, etc.).

sub_strands.name: 8.2 Religion and Social Change
specific_learning_outcomes: Analyse the role of religion in promoting social justice
Discuss Christian responses to poverty, corruption, and inequality
Initiate community service projects inspired by faith
suggested_learning_experiences: Study biblical teachings on justice (Micah 6:8, Isaiah 1:17).
Research historical figures like Martin Luther King Jr., Desmond Tutu, Mother Teresa.
Plan a community outreach program (feeding, clean-up, tutoring).

national_goals_of_education: Foster national unity, patriotism, and promote national cohesion.
Increase the level of knowledge of all people to enhance national capacity and strengthen the economy.
Affirm and promote the culture of all Kenyan people.
Provide education and training that enables learners to become self-reliant and contribute to national development.
Promote holistic well-being—physical, emotional, spiritual—for self-reliance.
Enable learners to recognize values, ethics, and morals from religious and cultural perspectives and integrate them with their environment.
Enable learners to use language efficiently as a communication, thinking, and learning tool.
Develop learners’ ability to seek, use, and disseminate information using communication technology.
Develop learners’ ability to solve problems using knowledge, skills, and attitudes.
Enable learners to recognize their potential and harness it for the benefit of family and society.
essence_statement: Education is a critical instrument for economic growth, national unity, and cultural affirmation. It aims to produce citizens capable of contributing to national development through knowledge, skills, and attitudes, while instilling values, ethics, and morals derived from religious and cultural heritage.
